land.name: Antelope
land.description: An extremely swift mammal found in open grasslands, featuring beautiful curved horns.

land.name: Badger
land.description: A stout, burrowing carnivore recognized by its striking black and white striped head.

land.name: Bat
land.description: The only mammal capable of true sustained flight, navigating using advanced echolocation.

land.name: Bear
land.description: A large, powerful omnivore with a thick coat of fur and heavy claws used for digging.

land.name: Bee
land.description: A crucial pollinator that lives in complex social colonies, producing sweet honey.

land.name: Beetle
land.description: An incredibly diverse insect with a hardened pair of front wings acting as a protective shell.

land.name: Bison
land.description: A massive hump-shouldered wild ox that once roamed North American plains in giant herds.

land.name: Boar
land.description: A wild ancestor of the domestic pig, equipped with sharp canine tusks and a tough bristly coat.

land.name: Butterfly
land.description: A beautiful insect with colorful, scale-covered wings that undergoes complete metamorphosis.

land.name: Cat
land.description: A small, agile domesticated carnivore prized by humans for companionship and pest control.

land.name: Caterpillar
land.description: The worm-like larval stage of butterflies and moths, possessing an insatiable appetite for leaves.

land.name: Chimpanzee
land.description: One of humanity's closest genetic relatives, highly intelligent and skilled in tool usage.

land.name: Cockroach
land.description: An incredibly resilient insect capable of surviving extreme conditions and environments.

land.name: Cow
land.description: A common domesticated herbivore raised globally for milk, meat, and agricultural labor.

land.name: Coyote
land.description: An adaptable North American canine known for its distinctive midnight howling and clever hunting.

land.name: Deer
land.description: A graceful hoofed herbivore, the males of which grow and shed branched antlers annually.

land.name: Dog
land.description: The first domesticated animal, bred into hundreds of unique shapes and sizes for work and companionship.

land.name: Donkey
land.description: A sturdy, patient pack animal related to horses, known for its long ears and distinct bray.

land.name: Dragonfly
land.description: An ancient aerial predator with large multifaceted eyes and four powerful wings that hover with agility.

land.name: Elephant
land.description: The largest living land animal, characterized by its long trunk, large ears, and ivory tusks.

land.name: Fly
land.description: A common insect with a single pair of flight wings and specialized sponge-like mouthparts.

land.name: Fox
land.description: An adaptable, clever carnivore with a bushy white-tipped tail and a sharp snout.

land.name: Goat
land.description: A hardy, sure-footed domesticated ruminant capable of climbing steep mountain faces.

land.name: Gorilla
land.description: A massive, powerful ape that lives in closely bonded family groups led by a silverback.

land.name: Grasshopper
land.description: A plant-eating insect equipped with powerful hind legs for jumping long distances.

land.name: Hamster
land.description: A small, nocturnal rodent featuring expandable cheek pouches used to carry food to its burrow.

land.name: Hare
land.description: A swift, long-eared mammal related to rabbits, living in simple nests rather than burrows.

land.name: Hedgehog
land.description: A small spiny mammal that rolls into a tight protective ball when threatened.

land.name: Hippopotamus
land.description: A massive semi-aquatic mammal, known for its territorial behavior and large canine tusks.

land.name: Horse
land.description: An elegant, powerful herd mammal domesticated by humans for transport and agriculture.

land.name: Hyena
land.description: A scavenger-predator with extremely strong jaws and a distinctive cackling laugh vocalization.

land.name: Kangaroo
land.description: A large hopping marsupial that raises its young in a front belly pouch.

land.name: Koala
land.description: An arboreal Australian marsupial that feeds almost exclusively on eucalyptus leaves.

land.name: Ladybug
land.description: A small dome-shaped beetle, red with black spots, prized by gardeners for eating pests.

land.name: Leopard
land.description: A solitary, athletic wild cat that drags its heavy prey high up into tree branches.

land.name: Lion
land.description: A majestic social cat known for the male's dark mane and coordinated pride hunting.

land.name: Lizard
land.description: A diverse reptile with a scaly skin, four legs, and the ability to shed its tail to escape predators.

land.name: Mosquito
land.description: A small fly whose females pierce skin to feed on blood to develop their eggs.

land.name: Moth
land.description: A nocturnal cousin of the butterfly, featuring feathered antennae and muted wing camouflages.

land.name: Mouse
land.description: A small, adaptable rodent with large rounded ears and a long hairless tail.

land.name: Okapi
land.description: A solitary forest animal related to giraffes, featuring zebra-like stripes on its legs.

land.name: Orangutan
land.description: A red-haired arboreal ape known for its long arms and intelligent nest-building habits.

land.name: Ox
land.description: A powerful, castrated domestic bull trained as a draft animal for pulling plows.

land.name: Panda
land.description: A beloved black-and-white bear that feeds almost exclusively on bamboo stalks.

land.name: Pig
land.description: An extremely intelligent domesticated mammal with an excellent sense of smell.

land.name: Porcupine
land.description: A slow-moving rodent covered in thousands of sharp, defensive barbed quills.

land.name: Possum
land.description: A nocturnal marsupial native to Australia, featuring a long prehensile tail.

land.name: Raccoon
land.description: A clever mammal with a black mask around its eyes and a ringed tail, famous for washing food.

land.name: Rat
land.description: An extremely adaptable, successful rodent with a long hairless tail and high intelligence.

land.name: Reindeer
land.description: An arctic deer species, where both males and females grow antlers, adapted to freezing cold.

land.name: Rhinoceros
land.description: A thick-skinned giant herbivore with one or two horns composed of compacted keratin.

land.name: Sheep
land.description: A domesticated woolly ruminant raised globally for meat, milk, and soft textile fibers.

land.name: Snake
land.description: A legless, scaly reptile that swallows prey whole and senses surroundings using a forked tongue.

land.name: Squirrel
land.description: A lively tree-dwelling rodent with a bushy tail, known for caching nuts for winter.

land.name: Tiger
land.description: The largest of all wild cats, recognized by its bold vertical dark stripes on orange fur.

land.name: Wolf
land.description: A pack-hunting wild canine with complex social hierarchies and critical ecosystem roles.

land.name: Wombat
land.description: A burrowing Australian marsupial known for its cube-shaped droppings and tough rear shield.

land.name: Zebra
land.description: A wild horse relative from Africa, famous for its unique vertical black-and-white stripes.

land.name: African Lion
land.description: A majestic social predator known for its impressive mane and powerful pride structure.

land.name: Bengal Tiger
land.description: The largest of all wild cats, recognized by its dark vertical stripes on reddish-orange fur.

land.name: African Elephant
land.description: The largest terrestrial mammal, boasting large ears, a long trunk, and prominent tusks.

land.name: Grizzly Bear
land.description: A powerful subspecies of brown bear with a distinctive shoulder hump and long claws.

land.name: Red Kangaroo
land.description: The largest marsupial, native to Australia, famous for its powerful hind legs and hopping locomotion.

land.name: Gray Wolf
land.description: A highly social pack predator with an essential role in maintaining ecological balance.

land.name: Giant Panda
land.description: A beloved black-and-white bear that feeds almost exclusively on bamboo shoots and leaves.

land.name: Reticulated Giraffe
land.description: The tallest living land animal, instantly recognizable by its long neck and patched coat.

land.name: Cheetah
land.description: The fastest land animal on Earth, built for short, high-speed sprints with a slender body.

land.name: White Rhinoceros
land.description: A giant herbivore with a wide mouth and two prominent horns made of keratin.

land.name: Mountain Gorilla
land.description: A critically endangered ape that lives in close-knit family groups led by a silverback.

land.name: Red Fox
land.description: An adaptable, clever predator found globally, featuring a bushy white-tipped tail.

land.name: Platypus
land.description: A unique egg-laying mammal with a duck-like bill, beaver-like tail, and otter-like webbed feet.

land.name: Fennec Fox
land.description: A small desert fox with oversized ears that help dissipate heat and locate prey underground.

land.name: Sloth
land.description: A famously slow-moving mammal that hosts green algae in its fur for camouflage.

land.name: Honey Badger
land.description: A fearless, thick-skinned creature known to fend off leopards, lions, and venomous snakes.

land.name: Capybara
land.description: The world's largest rodent, exceptionally social and excellent at swimming in rivers.

marine.name: Crab
marine.description: A decapod crustacean equipped with a thick exoskeleton and a pair of powerful pinching claws.

marine.name: Dolphin
marine.description: A highly intelligent semi-aquatic mammal known for its playful social behavior and echolocation.

marine.name: Goldfish
marine.description: A small, colorful domestic carp that has been selectively bred for aquariums for centuries.

marine.name: Jellyfish
marine.description: A gelatinous drift animal with stinging tentacles used to capture small fish and plankton.

marine.name: Lobster
marine.description: A bottom-dwelling marine crustacean with large, unequal claws used for capturing food.

marine.name: Octopus
marine.description: A highly intelligent, eight-armed cephalopod capable of advanced camouflage and problem solving.

marine.name: Otter
marine.description: A playful semi-aquatic mammal with dense waterproof fur and webbed feet.

marine.name: Oyster
marine.description: A bivalve mollusk that filters organic particles from water and can secrete pearls.

marine.name: Seahorse
marine.description: A small, unique marine fish with an upright posture, horse-like head, and prehensile tail.

marine.name: Seal
marine.description: A sleek semi-aquatic marine mammal with webbed flippers that swims with speed.

marine.name: Shark
marine.description: An ancient cartilaginous predator equipped with multiple rows of replaceable teeth.

marine.name: Squid
marine.description: A fast-swimming cephalopod with ten arms, large eyes, and a siphon used for jet propulsion.

marine.name: Starfish
marine.description: A star-shaped marine invertebrate capable of regenerating lost arms, feeding using a reversible stomach.

marine.name: Turtle
marine.description: An ancient reptile protected by a bony shell, with marine species possessing paddle-like flippers.

marine.name: Whale
marine.description: A colossal warm-blooded marine mammal that breathes air through blowholes.

marine.name: Blue Whale
marine.description: The largest creature ever known to have lived on Earth, feeding primarily on tiny krill.

marine.name: Humpback Whale
marine.description: Renowned for its spectacular breaching displays and complex, haunting vocal songs.

marine.name: Orca (Killer Whale)
marine.description: An apex predator and largest dolphin relative, hunting in highly organized family pods.

marine.name: Bottlenose Dolphin
marine.description: Highly intelligent marine mammals known for their playful behavior and echolocation.

marine.name: Sperm Whale
marine.description: A deep-diving leviathan that hunts giant squid in the pitch-black ocean depths.

marine.name: Beluga Whale
marine.description: A social white whale with a bulbous, flexible forehead used for communication.

marine.name: Narwhal
marine.description: The 'unicorn of the sea,' featuring a long spiral tusk which is actually an elongated tooth.

marine.name: Manta Ray
marine.description: A gentle giant ray that glides gracefully through water, filtering plankton with its mouth.

marine.name: Whale Shark
marine.description: The largest fish in the sea, decorated with a unique pattern of white spots and stripes.

marine.name: Great White Shark
marine.description: A formidable predator equipped with rows of serrated teeth and acute sensory organs.

birds.name: Crow
birds.description: An exceptionally intelligent bird known for problem-solving, memory, and complex communication.

birds.name: Duck
birds.description: A semi-aquatic waterfowl featuring webbed feet, a flat bill, and waterproof feathers.

birds.name: Eagle
birds.description: A powerful bird of prey possessing keen eyesight, sharp talons, and a hooked beak.

birds.name: Flamingo
birds.description: A tall, slender wading bird with pink feathers derived from carotenoid pigments in its diet.

birds.name: Goose
birds.description: A large migratory waterfowl known for its protective nesting habits and loud honking.

birds.name: Hornbill
birds.description: A tropical bird characterized by a long, down-curved bill which often displays a decorative casque.

birds.name: Hummingbird
birds.description: A tiny nectar feeder capable of hovering mid-air and flying backwards by beating wings rapidly.

birds.name: Owl
birds.description: A nocturnal bird of prey with large forward-facing eyes and silent flight feathers.

birds.name: Parrot
birds.description: A colorful, vocal bird known for its curved bill, intelligence, and mimicry of speech.

birds.name: Pelican
birds.description: A family of large waterbirds including pelicans, featuring throat pouches for scooping fish.

birds.name: Penguin
birds.description: A flightless, tuxedoed marine bird that glides through sub-zero waters to catch fish.

birds.name: Pigeon
birds.description: A stout-bodied bird with a cooing voice, highly adapted to urban environments globally.

birds.name: Sandpiper
birds.description: A small wading bird that runs along sandy beaches to peck at tiny shoreline invertebrates.

birds.name: Sparrow
birds.description: A small, common songbird with brown and grey feathers, nesting near human dwellings.

birds.name: Swan
birds.description: A large, elegant white waterfowl with a long neck, forming lifelong breeding pairs.

birds.name: Turkey
birds.description: A large forest bird native to North American woodlands, featuring a bare head and fan-shaped tail.

birds.name: Woodpecker
birds.description: A tree-climbing bird that taps trunks with its beak to find grubs and drums to communicate.

birds.name: Bald Eagle
birds.description: The national symbol of the USA, boasting a pure white head and a sharp yellow beak.

birds.name: Golden Eagle
birds.description: One of the largest, fastest, and most nimble birds of prey, capable of hunting young deer.

birds.name: Peregrine Falcon
birds.description: The fastest creature in the animal kingdom, diving at extreme speeds to catch prey mid-air.

birds.name: Barn Owl
birds.description: A nocturnal hunter with a heart-shaped facial disc that funnels sound to its sensitive ears.

birds.name: Snowy Owl
birds.description: A striking, white owl of the cold north, which hunts by day and night using sharp eyesight.
